articles.title: Brain-computer interface trials are taking off
articles.subtitle: This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell—a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. Harrell is paralyzed and unable to speak coherently without the device. He ha
articles.author: Jessica Hamzelou
articles.summary: This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell—a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. Harrell is paralyzed and unable to speak coherently without the device. He has now spent almost three years using a brain-computer interface (BCI) that enables…
body: The number of people with electrodes in their brains is believed to have more than doubled in the last couple of years.

This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell —a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. Harrell is paralyzed and unable to speak coherently without the device. He has now spent almost three years using a brain-computer interface (BCI) that enables him to “speak,” surf the web, and perform his job as a climate activist, largely independently.

Since Harrell was implanted with the device, in July 2023, a team at the University of California, Davis, has worked with him to adjust and improve its offerings. They’ve refined its accuracy, for example. And they’ve introduced settings including a privacy mode and a “profanity filter” that lets Harrell talk to his daughter without risking accidental swearing.
Harrell told me that, for him, the device is “nothing short of revolutionary!” It has enabled him to maintain an income, reconnect with friends and family, and read to his daughter.

The team that developed his BCI is one of several working on ways to use technology to allow people with paralysis to communicate, engage with the online world, and regain some independence. And Harrell is one of a growing number of people volunteering their brains to, as he puts it, “pay it forward and do the scientific research … [and] get some personal benefit.”

Over the past couple of years, the number of BCI trial volunteers has soared. This year, China became the first country to approve a BCI for medical use. Advances in technology are allowing engineers to provide more features than ever. BCI research is properly taking off.

I should first point out that BCIs come in different forms . Harrell’s device includes a set of electrodes embedded in his brain that pick up the electrical activity associated with speech. Those electrodes are connected to two docking ports on top of his head that can be plugged into a computer.

That computer is loaded with software trained to decode his brain signals into phonemes (units of sound in speech) and predict what Harrell wants to say. He can then use an eye gaze tracker to make any corrections before the speech is played out loud.

But some BCIs don’t need to be “plugged in”—they’re fully implanted and wireless. Others are less invasive; they might involve placing wired electrodes on the surface of the brain or simply wearing a cap of electrodes, for example. There are trade-offs—the closer you get to the neurons you want to record from, the better your signal will be. But generally speaking, the more invasive the surgery, the higher the risk of complications.

BCIs can also have different functions. Harrell has ALS, but most BCIs in use today are sitting in the brains of people with spinal cord injuries. Typically, these individuals have some degree of paralysis; for example, they may be unable to move their arms and legs, but their face and ability to speak are unaffected. In those cases, BCIs can be used to control other kinds of devices that might help with mobility.

In 2024, Michelle Patrick-Krueger, then at the University of Houston, and her colleagues published a roundup of all trials of BCIs conducted between 1998 , which is when they believe the first device was implanted, and the end of 2023. They identified 21 research groups that, among them, had trialed BCIs in a total of 67 volunteers.

“Since then, that number has increased a lot,” says Mariska Vansteensel, a BCI researcher at University Medical Center Utrecht. In January, Neuralink (the BCI company founded by trillionaire Elon Musk) announced that it has implanted 21 people with its device in the past two years.
keyPoints: The number of people with electrodes in their brains is believed to have more than doubled in the last couple of years.

This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell —a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. Harrell is paralyzed and unable to speak coherently without the device. He has now spent almost three years using a brain-computer interface (BCI) that enables him to “speak,” surf the web, and perform his job as a climate activist, largely independently.

Since Harrell was implanted with the device, in July 2023, a team at the University of California, Davis, has worked with him to adjust and improve its offerings. They’ve refined its accuracy, for example. And they’ve introduced settings including a privacy mode and a “profanity filter” that lets Harrell talk to his daughter without risking accidental swearing.
paragraphTranslations: 过去几年中，大脑中植入电极的人数被认为已经翻了一番多。
本周，我报道了凯西·哈雷尔的故事——他患有肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS），据与他合作的研究人员称，他是“第一个使用大脑植入物的高手”。哈雷尔瘫痪了，没有这个设备就无法清晰地说话。他现在几乎花了三年时间使用一种脑机接口（BCI），使他能够“说话”，上网冲浪，并主要独立地担任气候活动家的工作。
自从哈雷尔在2023年7月植入了这个设备以来，加州大学戴维斯分校的一个团队一直在与他合作，调整和改进其功能。例如，他们提高了其准确性。他们还引入了包括隐私模式和“亵渎过滤器”在内的设置，让哈雷尔在与女儿交谈时不会意外地说出脏话。
哈雷尔告诉我，对他来说，这个设备是“革命性的！”它使他能够维持收入，与朋友和家人重新建立联系，并给女儿读书。
开发他的BCI的团队是几个正在研究如何利用技术让瘫痪的人交流、参与在线世界并重获一些独立性的团队之一。哈雷尔是越来越多的自愿将大脑用于“向前推进并进行科学研究……[并]获得一些个人利益”的人之一。
在过去的几年里，BCI试验志愿者的数量激增。今年，中国成为第一个批准将BCI用于医疗用途的国家。技术的进步使工程师能够提供比以往更多的功能。BCI研究正在真正起飞。
我首先应该指出，BCI有不同形式。哈雷尔的设备包括一组嵌入他大脑中的电极，这些电极可以捕捉与言语相关的电活动。这些电极连接到他头顶上的两个对接端口，可以插入计算机。
那台计算机装有经过训练的软件，可以将他的大脑信号解码成音素（言语中的音单位），并预测哈雷尔想要说什么。然后他可以使用眼动追踪器在语音大声播放之前进行任何更正。
但有些BCI不需要“插入”——它们是完全植入的，而且是无线的。其他的侵入性较小；它们可能涉及将有线电极放置在大脑表面或简单地戴上一顶电极帽，例如。有权衡——你越接近你想要记录的神经元，你的信号就会越好。但一般来说，手术越侵入性，并发症的风险就越高。
BCI也可以有不同的功能。哈雷尔患有ALS，但今天使用的大多数BCI都在脊髓损伤的人的大脑中。通常情况下，这些人有一定程度的瘫痪；例如，他们可能无法移动他们的手臂和腿，但他们的面部和说话能力不受影响。在这些情况下，BCI可以用来控制其他可能有助于移动性的设备。
2024年，当时在休斯顿大学的米歇尔·帕特里克-克鲁格和她同事们发表了一份综述，总结了从1998年到2023年底进行的所有BCI试验，他们认为第一个设备是在1998年植入的。他们确定了21个研究小组，他们总共在67名志愿者中试验了BCI。
“从那时起，这个数字增加了很多，”乌得勒支大学医学中心的BCI研究员马里斯卡·范·斯特恩塞尔说。今年1月，Neuralink（由万亿富翁埃隆·马斯克创立的BCI公司）宣布在过去两年中已经有21人植入了它的设备。

paragraphs: The number of people with brain electrodes has more than doubled in recent years. Casey Harrell, an ALS patient, has been using a brain-computer interface (BCI) for nearly three years. This device allows him to 'speak', surf the web, and work as a climate activist. The University of California, Davis team has improved the device's accuracy and added privacy and profanity filters. Harrell considers the device revolutionary, helping him maintain income and reconnect with loved ones. The number of BCI trial volunteers has increased, and China approved a BCI for medical use this year. BCIs come in various forms, from fully implanted and wireless to less invasive surface electrode caps. They can help people with paralysis communicate and regain independence.

articles.title: Trump hits out at Italy's Meloni after pushback on G7 photo claim
articles.subtitle: Trump said the Italian leader asked "over and over" for a picture at G7, after she said the US president "made-up" a story that she "begged" for a photo.
articles.author: BBC World
articles.summary: Trump said the Italian leader asked "over and over" for a picture at G7, after she said the US president "made-up" a story that she "begged" for a photo.
body: US President Donald Trump has hit out at Italy's prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, repeating his claim that she asked "over and over" for a picture of them together at the G7 summit this week.
In a post on Truth Social, Trump also accused her of not supporting US efforts to "deny] Iran from obtaining or developing a nuclear weapon".
He said that Meloni caused "a great logistical inconvenience" by barring the US from using Italian air facilities for American military operations in Iran.
On Friday, the prime minister said she had been astonished by Trump's initial claim that she "begged" for a photo.
In his post on Truth Social a day later, the US president said Meloni was "doing poorly in Italy with her level of popularity".
"Now, after the United States defeated Iran militarily, she wants to be friends again in order to get her "numbers up." No thanks!!!," he added.
In March, Italy reportedly denied US military air craft from landing at Sigonella air base in Sicily for operations against Iran.
The continued exchange has highlighted a developing rift between the two countries since Trump's military action against Iran this year.
Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani has also cancelled a trip to the US early next week.

Trump and Meloni were pictured in close conversation at the G7 summit in France this week, and the Italian leader later told reporters their relationship was unchanged and there had been "no recriminations".
But soon afterwards, Trump gave a phone interview with Italy's La7 TV channel in which he alleged: "She begged me to take a photo with her; I felt sorry for her."
"She's probably happy I spoke to her," he said. La7 did not produce Trump's original words in English, but voiced them over in Italian.
keyPoints: US President Donald Trump has hit out at Italy's prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, repeating his claim that she asked "over and over" for a picture of them together at the G7 summit this week.
In a post on Truth Social, Trump also accused her of not supporting US efforts to "deny] Iran from obtaining or developing a nuclear weapon".
He said that Meloni caused "a great logistical inconvenience" by barring the US from using Italian air facilities for American military operations in Iran.
paragraphTranslations: 美国总统唐纳德·特朗普抨击意大利总理焦尔吉娅·梅洛尼，重申她“一次又一次”要求在本周G7峰会上与他合影。
在Truth Social上的一篇帖子中，特朗普还指责她不支持美国“阻止伊朗获得或发展核武器”的努力。
他说梅洛尼禁止美国使用意大利的空中设施进行对伊朗的美国军事行动，造成了“巨大的后勤不便”。
周五，总理表示，特朗普最初声称她“乞求”合影，她对此感到惊讶。
在美国总统在Truth Social上发表帖子的一天后，他说梅洛尼在意大利的受欢迎程度“表现不佳”。
“现在，在美国军事上击败伊朗后，她想再次成为朋友，以提高她的‘数字’。不客气！”，他补充道。
今年3月，意大利据报拒绝美国军用飞机在西西里岛的锡戈内拉空军基地降落，以对抗伊朗。
持续的交流突显了自特朗普今年对伊朗采取军事行动以来两国之间日益扩大的裂痕。
意大利外交部长安东尼奥·塔亚尼也取消了下周初访问美国的行程。
特朗普和梅洛尼本周在法国的G7峰会上被拍到在密切交谈，意大利领导人后来告诉记者，他们的关系没有变化，没有“指责”。
但不久之后，特朗普在接受意大利La7电视台的电话采访时声称：“她乞求我与她合影；我为她感到难过。”
“她可能很高兴我跟她说话，”他说。La7没有用英语播放特朗普的原话，而是用意大利语配音。

vocabulary.example: He criticized the government's new policy.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 他批评了政府的新政策。

vocabulary.example: She accused him of lying.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 她指责他撒谎。

vocabulary.example: The logistical support was inadequate.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 后勤支援不足。

vocabulary.example: The road closure caused a lot of inconvenience.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 道路封闭造成了很大的不便。

vocabulary.example: His popularity among students is high.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 他在学生中的受欢迎程度很高。

vocabulary.example: The team was defeated in the final match.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 这个队伍在决赛中被击败了。

paragraphs: US President Donald Trump criticized Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, claiming she repeatedly asked for a photo with him at the G7 summit. Trump also accused Meloni of not supporting US efforts to prevent Iran from developing nuclear weapons. He mentioned that Meloni had barred the US from using Italian air facilities for military operations in Iran, causing logistical inconvenience. Meloni expressed astonishment at Trump's initial claim that she 'begged' for a photo. Trump further stated that Meloni's popularity in Italy is low and that she wants to be friends with the US again after the US defeated Iran militarily to improve her popularity.
paragraphTranslations: 美国总统唐纳德·特朗普批评了意大利总理焦尔吉娅·梅洛尼，声称她在G7峰会上多次要求与他合影。特朗普还指责梅洛尼不支持美国阻止伊朗发展核武器的努力。他提到梅洛尼禁止美国使用意大利的空军设施进行针对伊朗的军事行动，造成了后勤不便。梅洛尼对特朗普最初的声称她“乞求”合影表示惊讶。特朗普进一步表示，梅洛尼在意大利的人气很低，她希望在美军击败伊朗后再次与美国成为朋友，以提高她的人气。
